title: Snap project configuration
description: Versioned, team-shared configuration for the Snap plugin (snap.config.json at project root).
$comment: The optional `remote` block holds non-secret locators provisioned for a remote backend (Notion db ids, Jira project key, GitHub project). Tokens never live here — they stay in .env (D-027).

docsPath.description: Root folder for product docs inside the repo.

ticketsPath.description: Root folder for delivery tickets (epics/stories/tasks/bugs) inside the repo.

providers.description: External source-of-truth providers per artifact class. A backend is EXCLUSIVE per class (no mirror/sync — D-027). null = not configured yet.

doc.description: Source of truth for the product docs (/define). repository = local Markdown; notion = exclusively remote (D-027).

tickets.description: Source of truth for the delivery backlog (/ticket). repository = local Markdown; github-projects/jira = exclusively remote board (D-033).

repository.description: Code host for the delivery skills (/develop). Not the ticket board — that is `tickets`.

wireframe.description: Behavior of the /wireframe skill. The active design tool is providers.wireframe; wireframes are authored in that tool through its MCP server (no in-repo artifact).

ds.description: Behavior of the /ds skill. The design system is authored in the active design tool (providers.design) through its MCP server (target: mcp); code is import source / export target only. tokenFormat is the canonical interchange format for code I/O; exportPath is where generated code lands in the user's app (null = ask).

design.description: Behavior of the /design skill. Hi-Fi mockups are composed from the /ds design system and authored in the active design tool (providers.design — the same key and MCP servers as /ds) through its MCP server (target: mcp); no in-repo artifact. interactive sets how far interactivity goes: static (default, complete styled boards, no links) or prototype (clickable prototype links following the user flow, with graceful degradation if the MCP can't author links).

develop.description: Behavior of the /develop skill (D-037). The code host is providers.repository (github/gitlab via gh/glab CLI); owner/repo are derived from the git remote, no secret here.

mode.description: Control flow. gate = stop for human approval after the plan, before writing code; autonomous = run through to a draft PR/MR with no stop. A run argument overrides this.

review.description: Behavior of the /review skill (D-038). Code review over a local diff or a PR/MR (routed by providers.repository). Read-only by default; only the --fix run argument writes, and only to the working tree or the PR branch.

dimensions.description: Review axes run in parallel (one model-typed agent each). Defaults to all four; a team can narrow the set.

tests.description: Behavior of the /tests skill (D-039). Writes tests from a ticket's acceptance criteria or a diff/PR, runs them, and loops to green — fixing only the tests, never the source. Coverage is acceptance-criteria coverage (not line coverage).

levels.description: Test levels written in parallel (one model-typed tester each). Defaults to all three; a team can narrow the set.

mode.description: Control flow for the write step. gate = present the test plan and stop for approval before writing; autonomous = write straight through. Absent → inherits develop.mode at runtime. A run argument overrides this.

maxIterations.description: Maximum write+run+fix iterations of the green loop before remaining test-bugs are recorded as test debt (they never affect the verdict).

qa.description: Behavior of the /qa skill (D-040). Validates a ticket's acceptance criteria against the RUNNING product by exercising each criterion live (web/api/cli), then emits a binary verdict (accepted|rejected) and transitions the ticket. Never edits the source; runs on a test/staging profile only (never prod). One pass — no internal loop (the retry loop belongs to /fulldev).

surfaces.description: Surfaces exercised in parallel (one validator each); each acceptance criterion is routed to a surface. Defaults to all three; a team can narrow the set.

mode.description: Control flow for the app boot. gate = present the QA plan and stop for approval BEFORE booting the app (side effects); autonomous = run straight through. Absent → inherits develop.mode at runtime. A run argument overrides this.

run.description: How to run the app under test (test/staging profile only — never prod). Optional: when absent, the run command is detected by snap-explorer and the base URL can be passed via --base-url. cmd = start command; url = base URL of an already-running instance (reuse, no boot/teardown); readyWhen = health path or readiness signal; teardown = explicit stop command (else the booted process group is killed).

fulldev.description: Behavior of the /fulldev orchestrator (D-041). Chains develop → tests → review → qa in a bounded loop until every gate is green or the budget is spent. It is the only looper; the sub-skills are single-pass verdict emitters. Never edits the source or the gates, never merges (the draft PR is left for a human).

mode.description: Control flow, OVERRIDING each sub-skill's own mode. gate = each sub-skill stops at its own gate for approval; autonomous = run the chain straight through. A run argument overrides this.

maxCycles.description: Global budget: the maximum number of /develop passes in a run before the loop stops (stopped-budget).

maxPerGate.description: Per-gate cap: the number of red verdicts a single gate (tests/review/qa) may return before it is blocked (no longer re-run); a blocked gate makes the global green unreachable (stopped-blocked).

remote.description: Non-secret locators provisioned for a remote backend (D-032/D-033). Written by /snap:init; read by the snap-loader / snap-writer agents. Tokens never live here (they stay in .env).

databases.description: Map of entity class → Notion database id (personas, features, decisions).

jira.description: Jira connection (providers.tickets = jira). Connects to an existing project; does not create one.
